clusters.title: Mourners chant for revenge as dayslong funeral of Iran's slain leader begins
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran war live: Huge crowds mourn Khamenei, Trump hints at Netanyahu meeting
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, NBC World and others

items.title: Mourners chant for revenge as dayslong funeral of Iran's slain leader begins
items.standfirst: The flag-draped casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was put on display in Tehran with millions expected to attend his dayslong funeral.
items.content: The flag-draped casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was put on display in Tehran with millions expected to attend his dayslong funeral.

items.title: Iran war live: Huge crowds mourn Khamenei, Trump hints at Netanyahu meeting
items.standfirst: Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and members of his family were killed in a US-Israeli air strike in February.
items.content: Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and members of his family were killed in a US-Israeli air strike in February.

items.title: Inside Tehran as funeral for late Iranian supreme leader
items.standfirst: NBC News’ Richard Engel is in Tehran where huge crowds are gathering for the funeral of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Our team was at the mosque where mourners were chanting “death to America.” The ayatollah was killed in American and Israeli airstrikes at the start of the war earlier this year.
items.content: NBC News’ Richard Engel is in Tehran where huge crowds are gathering for the funeral of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Our team was at the mosque where mourners were chanting “death to America.” The ayatollah was killed in American and Israeli airstrikes at the start of the war earlier this year.

items.title: Funeral of Iran's former supreme leader 'intensely political moment'
items.standfirst: Authorities expect up to 20 million people to attend ceremonies across Iran and Iraq over the coming days.
items.content: Authorities expect up to 20 million people to attend ceremonies across Iran and Iraq over the coming days.

items.title: Masses of Iranians defy heatwave on second day of Khamenei’s funeral
items.standfirst: Iran has marked the second day of funeral processions for its late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.
items.content: Iran has marked the second day of funeral processions for its late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

items.title: Hezbollah, Hamas and Houthi officials attend funeral ceremonies for Iran's Khamenei
items.standfirst: Tens of thousands of mourners flocked to Tehran's Grand Mosalla Mosque to pay their respects to late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Saturday. Among them were representatives from Hamas, Hezbollah and the Houthis of Yemen, members of the "axis of resistance" that Iran has long supported as part of its years-long struggle against Israel.
items.content: Tens of thousands of mourners flocked to Tehran's Grand Mosalla Mosque to pay their respects to late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Saturday. Among them were representatives from Hamas, Hezbollah and the Houthis of Yemen, members of the "axis of resistance" that Iran has long supported as part of its years-long struggle against Israel.

items.title: BBC in Tehran as mourners gather for former supreme leader's funeral
items.standfirst: Iranian authorities say they expect millions of people to take part in funeral commemorations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
items.content: Iranian authorities say they expect millions of people to take part in funeral commemorations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: Iran holds funeral for Ali Khamenei: Attendees 'hardline factions' of society, specialist says
items.standfirst: Hundreds of thousands of mourners gathered in Tehran on Saturday for the start of a dayslong funeral for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Tara Kangarlou, a global affairs journalist and author of The Heartbeat of Iran, said most attendees were "more emboldened, hardline factions of the society," making the crowd unrepresentative of broader public attitudes toward the regime.
items.content: Hundreds of thousands of mourners gathered in Tehran on Saturday for the start of a dayslong funeral for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Tara Kangarlou, a global affairs journalist and author of The Heartbeat of Iran, said most attendees were "more emboldened, hardline factions of the society," making the crowd unrepresentative of broader public attitudes toward the regime.

items.title: Crowds gather as six-day funeral for former Iranian supreme leader begins
items.standfirst: Up to 30 million people expected to attend delayed events for Ali Khamenei, killed at start of war with US and IsraelHuge crowds have gathered at the funeral of the former Iranian supreme leader after the gates of the sprawling Grand Mosalla mosque in central Tehran let in thousands of mourners who had been waiting through the night to enter the grounds.Iran is staging mass funeral processions for Ali Khamenei – whose ⁠37-year reign was brought ⁠to an ​end in February by the first airstrike of the war launched by the US and Israel. By 5.30am, the Tehran streets surrounding the mosque were already filling up as Iranians, some travelling for hours and many carrying flags or posters of Khamenei, made their way to an event designed to emphasise the country’s sense of loss at the killing of the supreme leader and desire for revenge. Emotions filled the air as the crowds chanted Death to America and Israel. Continue reading...
items.content: Up to 30 million people expected to attend delayed events for Ali Khamenei, killed at start of war with US and IsraelHuge crowds have gathered at the funeral of the former Iranian supreme leader after the gates of the sprawling Grand Mosalla mosque in central Tehran let in thousands of mourners who had been waiting through the night to enter the grounds.Iran is staging mass funeral processions for Ali Khamenei – whose ⁠37-year reign was brought ⁠to an ​end in February by the first airstrike of the war launched by the US and Israel. By 5.30am, the Tehran streets surrounding the mosque were already filling up as Iranians, some travelling for hours and many carrying flags or posters of Khamenei, made their way to an event designed to emphasise the country’s sense of loss at the killing of the supreme leader and desire for revenge. Emotions filled the air as the crowds chanted Death to America and Israel. Continue reading...

items.title: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
items.standfirst: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.
items.content: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

items.title: Thousands gather for funeral of Iran's assassinated supreme leader
items.standfirst: A days-long funeral is under way in Tehran for Iran's former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
items.content: A days-long funeral is under way in Tehran for Iran's former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: Dayslong funeral for slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei begins in Tehran
items.standfirst: Khamenei, who ruled Iran for more than three decades, was killed in an airstrike on Feb. 28 at the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
items.content: Khamenei, who ruled Iran for more than three decades, was killed in an airstrike on Feb. 28 at the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)

items.title: Iran says millions expected at funeral for former supreme leader
items.standfirst: Foreign dignitaries and senior Iranian leaders honored Ayatollah Ali Khamenei today, months after he was killed by the United States and Israel. Iran says organizers expect 15 to 20 million people at his dayslong state funeral. NBC News' Richard Engel reports from Tehran.
items.content: Foreign dignitaries and senior Iranian leaders honored Ayatollah Ali Khamenei today, months after he was killed by the United States and Israel. Iran says organizers expect 15 to 20 million people at his dayslong state funeral. NBC News' Richard Engel reports from Tehran.

items.title: Ali Khamenei’s six-day funeral expected to draw millions in Iran
items.standfirst: Country’s leadership vows to never surrender as memorial on grand scale aims to relay message of resistance to worldIn the small hours of Friday the police roadblocks, stalls, posters and army vans were starting to appear across Tehran, as millions of Iranians prepared to attend the long-delayed six-day funeral ceremony for Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader for 36 turbulent years.Khamenei was killed aged 86 in the opening salvo of the US-Israeli attack on the country in February, and the final farewell ceremony is intended to be an epic display of personal mourning, national power, resilience and social cohesion. By Thursday, knots of mourners carrying flags and blankets were already gathering along roads festooned with banners showing the red fist, the symbol of the funeral, alongside the slogan: “We must rise.” Many were heading to special hostels being set up across Tehran for the pilgrims. In Revolution Square a giant statue of a clenched fist was being installed. Continue reading...
items.content: Country’s leadership vows to never surrender as memorial on grand scale aims to relay message of resistance to worldIn the small hours of Friday the police roadblocks, stalls, posters and army vans were starting to appear across Tehran, as millions of Iranians prepared to attend the long-delayed six-day funeral ceremony for Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader for 36 turbulent years.Khamenei was killed aged 86 in the opening salvo of the US-Israeli attack on the country in February, and the final farewell ceremony is intended to be an epic display of personal mourning, national power, resilience and social cohesion. By Thursday, knots of mourners carrying flags and blankets were already gathering along roads festooned with banners showing the red fist, the symbol of the funeral, alongside the slogan: “We must rise.” Many were heading to special hostels being set up across Tehran for the pilgrims. In Revolution Square a giant statue of a clenched fist was being installed. Continue reading...

items.title: Millions expected at delayed Khamenei funeral as Iran seeks to project strength
items.standfirst: Iran says millions are expected to turn out for the funeral, which will serve as a grand religious and political spectacle months after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed.
items.content: Iran says millions are expected to turn out for the funeral, which will serve as a grand religious and political spectacle months after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed.

clusters.title: Trump touts American "golden age", attacks communism in July 4th speech
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump lauds Americans in storm-delayed 250th anniversary speech
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, France24, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Trump touts American "golden age", attacks communism in July 4th speech
items.standfirst: In a speech delayed by a two-hour evacuation of the National Mall due to severe weather, President Trump mixed politics with history.
items.content: In a speech delayed by a two-hour evacuation of the National Mall due to severe weather, President Trump mixed politics with history.

items.title: Trump blasts 'communists', celebrates the US in 250th anniversary speech
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Saturday hailed America as the "crowning achievement of human history", in his speech celebrating 250 years of US independence in Washington. Trump claimed that under his presidency the United States was "prouder than ever before, even as he used the event to renew his attack on domestic opponents he branded communists.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Saturday hailed America as the "crowning achievement of human history", in his speech celebrating 250 years of US independence in Washington. Trump claimed that under his presidency the United States was "prouder than ever before, even as he used the event to renew his attack on domestic opponents he branded communists.

items.title: Trump lauds Americans in storm-delayed 250th anniversary speech
items.standfirst: President Trump has praised Americans as ‘a historic and heroic people’, in a speech on the nation's 250th anniversary.
items.content: President Trump has praised Americans as ‘a historic and heroic people’, in a speech on the nation's 250th anniversary.

items.title: US marks its 250th birthday with fireworks, flyovers and extreme weather
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump's speech included some of his political agenda but also honoured war veterans and American history.
items.content: US President Donald Trump's speech included some of his political agenda but also honoured war veterans and American history.

items.title: Trump speaks after hours-long delay for Freedom 250 event due to severe weather
items.standfirst: President Trump spoke in Washington, D.C., at the "Salute to America" Freedom 250 event on the Fourth of July. He highlighted veterans, the Artemis II crew, an early version of the American flag and more in his speech.
items.content: President Trump spoke in Washington, D.C., at the "Salute to America" Freedom 250 event on the Fourth of July. He highlighted veterans, the Artemis II crew, an early version of the American flag and more in his speech.

items.title: Donald Trump defies storms and heatwave to deliver July 4 address
items.standfirst: President eulogises forebears and eviscerates enemies at Lincoln Memorial on 250th anniversary of independence
items.content: President eulogises forebears and eviscerates enemies at Lincoln Memorial on 250th anniversary of independence

items.title: Trump delivers ominous warning to political opponents at America 250 event
items.standfirst: On Saturday, President Trump is slated to speak on the National Mall, but on Friday, he gave a speech at Mount Rushmore that jumped from praise for America to a warning about what he called a threat to its future. Nikole Killion reports.
items.content: On Saturday, President Trump is slated to speak on the National Mall, but on Friday, he gave a speech at Mount Rushmore that jumped from praise for America to a warning about what he called a threat to its future. Nikole Killion reports.

items.title: In Mount Rushmore speech, Trump veers from U.S. exceptionalism to warnings about communism
items.standfirst: President Trump ushered in America's 250th anniversary with a darkly political speech that swerved from the typically apolitical, unifying speeches past presidents have given to mark Independence Day.
items.content: President Trump ushered in America's 250th anniversary with a darkly political speech that swerved from the typically apolitical, unifying speeches past presidents have given to mark Independence Day.(Image credit: Alex Brandon)

items.title: Trump addresses nation and fireworks light up National Mall after storm delay
items.standfirst: President Trump lauded America's storied history on Saturday night in a speech marking the 250th anniversary of its independence, while also praising his war in Iran, railing against "communists," and vowing to restrict mail-in ballots.
items.content: President Trump lauded America's storied history on Saturday night in a speech marking the 250th anniversary of its independence, while also praising his war in Iran, railing against "communists," and vowing to restrict mail-in ballots. (Image credit: Jose Luis Magana)

items.title: A divided US celebrates 250th birthday as heatwave bears down on East Coast
items.standfirst: The US is celebrating the 250th anniversary of the country's independence on Saturday under sweltering temperatures, with a massive fireworks show planned in Washington following a speech by US President Donald Trump.
items.content: The US is celebrating the 250th anniversary of the country's independence on Saturday under sweltering temperatures, with a massive fireworks show planned in Washington following a speech by US President Donald Trump.

clusters.title: Major Ukrainian drone attack on Russia strikes St Petersburg oil terminal
clusters.neutral_headline: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg oil terminal and nearby port
bullet_summary: The St Petersburg governor, Alexander Beglov, said the city had been subjected to a “large-scale” drone attack that had hit its oil terminal
He said there were no casualties and the aftermath of the attack had been dealt with

items.title: Major Ukrainian drone attack on Russia strikes St Petersburg oil terminal
items.standfirst: Ukrainian drones have struck an oil terminal and port in St Petersburg and the surrounding region, Russian and Ukrainian officials say.
items.content: Ukrainian drones have struck an oil terminal and port in St Petersburg and the surrounding region, Russian and Ukrainian officials say.

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg oil terminal and nearby port
items.standfirst: St Petersburg governor reports no victims after ‘large-scale’ overnight attack that also hit Baltic port of VysotskUkraine launched a big overnight drone attack on St Petersburg and the surrounding area, hitting the city’s oil terminal and port infrastructure in the wider region.The St Petersburg governor, Alexander Beglov, said the city had been subjected to a “large-scale” drone attack that had hit its oil terminal. He said there were no casualties and the aftermath of the attack had been dealt with. Continue reading...

He said there were no casualties and the aftermath of the attack had been dealt with. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Curtis Young on America's "diffcult moment in the mirror"
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Reuters Top News

items.title: Curtis Young on America's "diffcult moment in the mirror"
items.standfirst: This weekend, the United States marks 250 years since the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, one of the most influential political texts in modern history. Its assertion that governments derive their power from the consent of the governed, and that all people are endowed with the rights to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness, has inspired democratic movements around the world.
items.content: This weekend, the United States marks 250 years since the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, one of the most influential political texts in modern history. Its assertion that governments derive their power from the consent of the governed, and that all people are endowed with the rights to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness, has inspired democratic movements around the world.

items.title: On the Declaration of Independence
items.standfirst: A July 4 reflection on the Declaration’s radical language and global significance, arguing that its claims about equality, unalienable rights, consent of the governed, and the right to alter or abolish unjust government helped reshape modern politics far beyond the United States. [acoup.blog]
items.content: A July 4 reflection on the Declaration’s radical language and global significance, arguing that its claims about equality, unalienable rights, consent of the governed, and the right to alter or abolish unjust government helped reshape modern politics far beyond the United States. [acoup.blog]

items.title: Xi Replaces Anti-Corruption Leader in Purge of China’s Army
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping has appointed a new head of anti-corruption efforts in the armed forces as he continues the country’s biggest military purge in half a century.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping has appointed a new head of anti-corruption efforts in the armed forces as he continues the country’s biggest military purge in half a century.
